transcript.speaker: agent
transcript.text: Hello, support.

transcript.speaker: customer
transcript.text: Hi, I'm calling because I can't log into my account. I've been trying all morning and it keeps saying invalid password, but I know my password is correct.

transcript.speaker: agent
transcript.text: Okay, have you tried resetting your password?

transcript.speaker: customer
transcript.text: Well no, I shouldn't have to. The password was working yesterday. Something changed on your end.

transcript.speaker: agent
transcript.text: Yeah so the easiest thing is to just reset your password. Go to the login page and click forgot password.

transcript.speaker: customer
transcript.text: But I don't want to reset it. Can you check if something happened to my account? I'm worried it might have been hacked or something.

transcript.speaker: agent
transcript.text: I can't really check that without pulling up your account. What's your email?

transcript.speaker: customer
transcript.text: It's maria.gonzalez@outlook.com.

transcript.speaker: agent
transcript.text: Okay I see your account. It looks like your account got locked because of too many failed login attempts.

transcript.speaker: customer
transcript.text: What? I only tried like three times. How many does it take?

transcript.speaker: agent
transcript.text: I think it's five. But it shows eight attempts on your account. Could be someone else was trying to get in.

transcript.speaker: customer
transcript.text: That's really concerning. Is my account safe? Has anyone accessed it?

transcript.speaker: agent
transcript.text: I mean, the lock kicked in so probably not. You should be fine.

transcript.speaker: customer
transcript.text: Probably? Can you actually verify that? I have payment information stored in there.

transcript.speaker: agent
transcript.text: I don't have access to that level of detail. You'd need to talk to the security team for that. I can unlock your account though.

transcript.speaker: customer
transcript.text: Okay, please unlock it and connect me to the security team then.

transcript.speaker: agent
transcript.text: I've unlocked it. For the security team you'll need to email security@company.com, we don't do transfers to that department.

transcript.speaker: customer
transcript.text: So you can't transfer me? I have to email them separately?

transcript.speaker: agent
transcript.text: Yeah, that's how it works. Anything else?

transcript.speaker: customer
transcript.text: I guess not. This wasn't very reassuring.

transcript.speaker: agent
transcript.text: Sorry about that. Bye.
